_run.note: One reviewer per dimension (correctness / robustness / docs). This run found a REAL block-level bug, since fixed.

fails.dimension: conservative bias upheld (false 'safe' must never happen)
fails.evidence: Whole-tree tokens '', '.', '*', '**' and bare-directory paths were declared disjoint from specific paths: ['src'] vs ['src/foo.js'] => safe:true. A subagent owning the whole tree would be cleared to run in parallel with one owning a single file — corrupting a parallel write.

concerns_summary: executorBrief accepted an empty validation_commands array (inconsistent with the non-empty exclusive_write guard).
ownership.js header described prefix/equality matching but the code also had an undocumented wildcard heuristic.

resolution.commit: see git log after this example
fixes: Rewrote ownership.js matcher: prefix-based with a `whole` flag; '', '.', '*', '**' and leading-wildcard patterns now conflict with everything. The four false-safe cases now correctly report CONFLICT; genuinely disjoint sets stay safe.
executorBrief now rejects an empty validation_commands array.
Rewrote the ownership.js header to match the implemented rule.
Added tools/test-lib.mjs with regression tests for every false-safe case; wired into tools/check.sh (step 0/5) and CI.
